query: single-file components vs global components in Vue

competitors.label: Single-File Components
competitors.selectionReason: Vue SFC docs are the canonical baseline for single-file component structure and purpose.
theirStrengths: Single-File Components is a direct, credible comparator for this topic.
theirGaps: Single-File Components is less compressed around the specific interview framing and likely follow-up confusion this page targets.

realWorldUseCases:
Overview

Vue components can be created as Single-File Components (SFCs) or Global Components, but they optimize for different trade-offs. The production pitfall is registering too much globally: you save imports, but lose clear ownership, dependency tracing, and bundle discipline.
actionableExamples:
Single-File Components (SFCs)
Single-File Components are stored in .vue files and include three main sections — <template>, <script>, and <style>. They provide a modular, maintainable way to build complex applications.
followUpConfusion:
